justification: All 7 expected rules identified (BR-1 through BR-8, with BR-5 as a bonus rule). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day notice (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities surfaced formally via escalations (partial month accrual, calendar vs working days, cancellation, jurisdiction forfeiture). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 72 is appropriately calibrated given the ambiguities identified.

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant: true/false classification — no misclassifications
Invariant justifications are exceptionally well-reasoned, clearly distinguishing data integrity constraints from configurable policy
12 acceptance criteria with concrete numeric examples, covering accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical exception, rejection, contract boundary, and carryover
Rich ambiguity surfacing: partial-month accrual, working vs calendar days for leave counting, cancellation workflow, jurisdiction-specific forfeiture — all with options and recommendations
Bonus BR-5 (manager authorization) correctly classified as invariant: true with sound access-control reasoning
Edge cases section is thorough, including concurrency (double-deduction), contract date changes, and pending requests during carry-over
Explicitly surfaces the '5-day window: calendar vs working days' ambiguity called out in the rubric
Business impact analysis identifies financial, operational, and legal dimensions with specific stakeholder mapping
Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than distinguishing 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as separate bounded contexts per the rubric expectation
Did not explicitly surface the 'in-flight leave requests if contract ends mid-period' ambiguity as a distinct escalation, though the related edge case about contract date changes partially covers it
